hostname.description: Enter your Databricks workspace server hostname (e.g., abc-12345678-wxyz.cloud.databricks.com). You can find this in your cluster or SQL warehouse connection details.
hostname.title: Server Hostname

http_path.description: Enter the HTTP path for your Databricks SQL warehouse or cluster. You can find this in the connection details of your compute resource.
http_path.title: HTTP Path

port.description: Enter the port number for the Databricks cluster connection. The default port is 443 (HTTPS).
port.title: Port

database.description: Enter the name of the Unity Catalog that you want to sync data into.
database.title: Databricks Unity Catalog Name

schema.description: Enter the name of the default schema where tables will be written. If not specified, "default" will be used.
schema.title: Default Schema

cdc_deletion_mode.description: Whether to execute CDC deletions as hard deletes (i.e. propagate source deletions to the destination), or soft deletes (i.e. leave a tombstone record in the destination). Defaults to hard deletes.
cdc_deletion_mode.title: CDC deletion mode

client_id.description: Enter the OAuth2 client ID for your Databricks service principal.
client_id.title: Client ID

secret.description: Enter the OAuth2 secret associated with the client ID.
secret.title: Secret

oneOf.title: Personal Access Token
oneOf.description: Authentication using a personal access token.

authentication.description: Determines the type of authentication used to connect to Databricks. OAuth2 is recommended for production use.
authentication.title: Authentication

purge_staging_data.description: Whether to delete staging files and tables after data has been loaded. Disable this option for debugging purposes.
purge_staging_data.title: Purge Staging Files and Tables

accept_terms.description: You must agree to the Databricks JDBC Driver Terms & Conditions to use this connector.
accept_terms.title: Agree to the Databricks JDBC Driver Terms & Conditions
